deep_dive.title: Single-cell multi-omic atlas and morphogen screening informs midbrain and hindbrain organoid engineering.
deep_dive.abstract: Patterning of the neural tube establishes midbrain and hindbrain structures that coordinate motor movement, process sensory input and integrate cognitive functions. Cellular impairment within these structures underlies diverse neurological disorders, and in vitro organoid models promise inroads to understanding development and modeling disease. Here, we use paired single-cell transcriptome and accessible chromatin sequencing to map cell composition and regulatory mechanisms in organoid models of midbrain and hindbrain. We find that existing midbrain organoid protocols generate ventral and dorsal cell types, covering regions including floor plate, dorsal and ventral midbrain and adjacent hindbrain regions. Gene regulatory network inference and transcription factor perturbation resolve mechanisms underlying neuronal differentiation. A single-cell multiplexed patterning screen identifies morphogen concentrations that expand existing organoid models, including conditions generating medulla glycinergic neurons and cerebellum glutamatergic subtypes. Together, the multi-omic atlas and morphogen screen reveal morphogen-regulon relationships guiding region-specific progenitor differentiation towards diverse neuron types of the posterior brain.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: single-cell multi-omic atlas of midbrain and hindbrain organoid engineering
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 中脑与后脑类器官工程的单细胞多组学图谱
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 中脑与后脑类器官工程的单细胞多组学图谱
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 中脑与后脑类器官工程的单细胞多组学图谱
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper uses paired single-cell transcriptome and chromatin accessibility profiling to map cell composition and regulatory programs in midbrain and hindbrain organoid models, revealing that existing protocols generate mixed ventral/dorsal cell types and informing improved organoid engineering strategies for neurodevelopmental disease modeling.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究针对现有中脑类器官模型细胞组成与调控机制不明确的问题，采用配对的单细胞转录组和染色质可及性测序技术，系统绘制了中脑与后脑类器官的细胞类型图谱。结果显示，现有中脑类器官分化方案会同时产生腹侧和背侧细胞类型，覆盖底板、背侧与腹侧中脑及邻近后脑区域，并揭示了相关基因调控网络。该工作为优化中脑类器官分化方案、构建更精确的神经发育与疾病模型提供了单细胞水平的分子基础，对神经类器官工程化具有重要指导意义。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该研究针对现有中脑类器官模型细胞组成与调控机制不明确的问题，采用配对的单细胞转录组和染色质可及性测序技术，系统绘制了中脑与后脑类器官的细胞类型图谱。结果显示，现有中脑类器官分化方案会同时产生腹侧和背侧细胞类型，覆盖底板、背侧与腹侧中脑及邻近后脑区域，并揭示了相关基因调控网络。该工作为优化中脑类器官分化方案、构建更精确的神经发育与疾病模型提供了单细胞水平的分子基础，对神经类器官工程化具有重要指导意义。

deep_dive.title: Connecting transcriptional control to RNA velocity and cell fate.
deep_dive.abstract: RNA velocity can predict the direction of cell-state change, but the regulatory programs shaping these changes remain difficult to identify. In this issue of Cell, Wang et al. introduce RegVelo, a framework that integrates gene regulatory networks into RNA velocity analysis to nominate candidate regulators that may bias cell fate.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: RegVelo integrates gene regulatory networks into RNA velocity analysis
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: RegVelo将基因调控网络整合进RNA速率分析框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: RegVelo将基因调控网络整合进RNA速率分析框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: RegVelo将基因调控网络整合进RNA速率分析框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This piece addresses the gap between RNA velocity predictions and the regulatory programs driving cell-state transitions. It highlights RegVelo, a new framework by Wang et al. that integrates gene regulatory networks into RNA velocity analysis. This enables nomination of candidate regulators biasing cell fate, advancing computational tools for developmental trajectory inference.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该文章针对RNA速率分析虽能预测细胞状态变化方向、但难以识别背后调控程序的问题。作者介绍Wang等人提出的RegVelo框架，将基因调控网络整合进RNA速率分析中，以推断影响细胞命运的候选调控因子。该方法在Cell杂志相关研究中被证明能够有效连接转录调控与细胞状态转变预测。这一进展为单细胞轨迹推断和发育生物学分析提供了更具机制解释力的新工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该文章针对RNA速率分析虽能预测细胞状态变化方向、但难以识别背后调控程序的问题。作者介绍Wang等人提出的RegVelo框架，将基因调控网络整合进RNA速率分析中，以推断影响细胞命运的候选调控因子。该方法在Cell杂志相关研究中被证明能够有效连接转录调控与细胞状态转变预测。这一进展为单细胞轨迹推断和发育生物学分析提供了更具机制解释力的新工具。

deep_dive.title: Mitochondrial DNA heteroplasmy drives cortical neuronal disturbances in human organoids harbouring the common m.3243A>G mutation.
deep_dive.abstract: Mitochondrial diseases frequently affect the brain leading to severe and disabling neurological symptoms. The heteroplasmic m.3243 A > G mutation in MT-TL1, encoding mt-tRNALeu, is responsible for ~80% of mitochondrial encephalomyopathy, lactic acidosis, and stroke-like episodes (MELAS), which is one of the most characteristic mitochondrial syndromes, leading to disability and early death. There are no animal models harbouring this mutation to provide precise mechanistic insights informing therapeutic interventions. Here, we generate a human iPSC-derived cerebral organoid slice model that recapitulates cortical architecture and mitochondrial pathology. Using biological assays and single-cell RNA sequencing, we uncover heteroplasmy-dependent transcriptional shifts and changes in key cellular processes in cortical neurons. Organoids with high heteroplasmy show a predominant impairment of deep-layer neurons triggered by mitochondrial stress, leading to axonal degeneration and apoptosis, similar to brain autopsy of a MELAS patient. Our findings provide insights into the vulnerability of long-range projection neurons in mitochondrial diseases, advancing our understanding of disease mechanisms with a view to potential therapeutic strategies.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: human iPSC-derived cerebral organoid slice model recapitulating cortical architecture
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 人iPSC来源脑类器官切片模型重现皮层结构
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 人iPSC来源脑类器官切片模型重现皮层结构
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 人iPSC来源脑类器官切片模型重现皮层结构
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This study addresses the lack of models for the m.3243A>G mitochondrial mutation causing MELAS-related brain disease. Researchers built a human iPSC-derived cerebral organoid slice model recapitulating cortical architecture and used single-cell RNA sequencing to characterize mitochondrial pathology. Results reveal cortical neuronal disturbances driven by mitochondrial DNA heteroplasmy, offering mechanistic insight for mitochondrial encephalopathy research.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究针对携带常见m.3243A>G突变的线粒体脑病缺乏精确机制模型的问题。作者构建了人iPSC来源的脑类器官切片模型，重现皮层结构以及线粒体病理特征。通过生物学检测与单细胞RNA测序，发现线粒体DNA异质性导致皮层神经元功能紊乱。该研究为理解线粒体疾病相关神经病变提供了新的人源类器官模型和潜在治疗干预靶点。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该研究针对携带常见m.3243A>G突变的线粒体脑病缺乏精确机制模型的问题。作者构建了人iPSC来源的脑类器官切片模型，重现皮层结构以及线粒体病理特征。通过生物学检测与单细胞RNA测序，发现线粒体DNA异质性导致皮层神经元功能紊乱。该研究为理解线粒体疾病相关神经病变提供了新的人源类器官模型和潜在治疗干预靶点。

deep_dive.title: Soluble α2δ-1, altered in disease CSF, modulates network homeostasis and rescues deficits in a neuropsychiatric mouse model.
deep_dive.abstract: Excitation-inhibition (E/I) balance depends on dynamic communication between neuronal subtypes, potentially beyond classical neurotransmission. While membrane-bound ion channels are essential for neuronal function, their potential roles as extracellular regulators of network dynamics remain largely unexplored. Here, we identify a soluble form of the voltage-gated Ca2+ channel subunit α2δ-1 in human cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) and show that it acts as an activity-regulated intercellular modulator of network homeostasis. Soluble α2δ-1 is reduced in the CSF of individuals with schizophrenia (SZ). Its synthetic analog, synthetic ectodomain of Alpha2Delta-1 (SEAD1), modulates cortical activity by enhancing the function of parvalbumin-positive (PV+) interneurons and restoring E/I balance. A single SEAD1 injection into the prefrontal cortex of a genetic mouse model of SZ reversed synaptic and behavioral deficits, including memory and social impairments. These findings reveal soluble synaptic ectodomains as a previously underappreciated class of extracellular signaling molecules with therapeutic potential in neuropsychiatric disorders.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: soluble alpha2delta-1 modulates E/I network homeostasis and is altered in schizophrenia CSF, rescuing deficits in a neuropsychiatric mouse model
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 可溶性α2δ-1调控兴奋-抑制网络稳态，在精神分裂症CSF中改变，并在神经精神小鼠模型中挽救缺陷
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 可溶性α2δ-1调控兴奋-抑制网络稳态，在精神分裂症CSF中改变，并在神经精神小鼠模型中挽救缺陷
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 可溶性α2δ-1调控兴奋-抑制网络稳态，在精神分裂症CSF中改变，并在神经精神小鼠模型中挽救缺陷
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This study identifies a soluble form of calcium channel subunit alpha2delta-1 in human CSF that regulates cortical network excitation-inhibition homeostasis; it is reduced in schizophrenia patients, and its synthetic analog SEAD1 rescues circuit deficits in a neuropsychiatric mouse model, revealing a new extracellular modulator relevant to psychiatric disease mechanisms.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究关注精神分裂症相关的兴奋-抑制网络失衡机制问题。作者发现钙通道亚基α2δ-1的可溶性形式存在于人脑脊液中，作为活动依赖的细胞间调节因子维持皮层网络稳态；该分子在精神分裂症患者CSF中水平降低。研究进一步利用其合成类似物SEAD1在神经精神小鼠模型中挽救了皮层活动缺陷，实验结果表明该通路可能是干预E/I失衡的新靶点。这为理解精神分裂症等精神疾病的突触与环路机制提供了新的分子线索，具有潜在治疗意义。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该研究关注精神分裂症相关的兴奋-抑制网络失衡机制问题。作者发现钙通道亚基α2δ-1的可溶性形式存在于人脑脊液中，作为活动依赖的细胞间调节因子维持皮层网络稳态；该分子在精神分裂症患者CSF中水平降低。研究进一步利用其合成类似物SEAD1在神经精神小鼠模型中挽救了皮层活动缺陷，实验结果表明该通路可能是干预E/I失衡的新靶点。这为理解精神分裂症等精神疾病的突触与环路机制提供了新的分子线索，具有潜在治疗意义。

deep_dive.title: A tunable Cas12a platform for single-cell perturbation screening and CRISPRi.
deep_dive.abstract: Single-cell perturbation (Perturb-seq) screens have primarily relied on Cas9 for inducing loss-of-function phenotypes, whereas Cas12a, despite its unique effectiveness for multiplex guide expression, remains underexplored. This may be due to Cas12a's guide RNA array (pre-crRNA) self-processing activity and the subsequent challenges associated with pre-crRNA sequence recovery during single-cell RNA sequencing library preparation. To overcome the self-processing constraint, we optimized pre-crRNA expression vectors and established a degron-based, enhanced Cas12a system for gene knock-out. As demonstrated across cell types, target genes, and with a minimized guide RNA library, this platform allows for accurate detection of pre-crRNAs and gene editing-induced effects on the transcriptome in single cells. Additionally, we show that HyperLbCas12a outperforms other existing variants for multiplexed gene suppression. While the rapid reversibility of this repressor highlights specific kinetic constraints for degron-based single-cell recording, the system provides a potent, modular tool for contexts requiring tunable, transient silencing. Together, this suite of technologies greatly expands the possibilities for future Perturb-seq efforts and broader application of Cas12a for genetic disruption at scale.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Cas12a platform for single-cell Perturb-seq screening and CRISPRi
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 开发用于单细胞Perturb-seq筛选与CRISPRi的Cas12a平台
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 开发用于单细胞Perturb-seq筛选与CRISPRi的Cas12a平台
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 开发用于单细胞Perturb-seq筛选与CRISPRi的Cas12a平台
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper addresses limits of Cas9-based Perturb-seq by engineering a Cas12a system with optimized pre-crRNA processing for multiplexed single-cell perturbation screens. Tested across cell types and genes, it enables accurate pre-crRNA detection and gene editing with minimized guide libraries, expanding single-cell perturbation toolkits.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有单细胞扰动筛选（Perturb-seq）主要依赖Cas9，而Cas12a虽适合多重向导RNA表达却因pre-crRNA自我加工特性而难以在单细胞RNA测序中准确恢复序列。本文通过优化pre-crRNA表达载体并构建基于降解子的增强型Cas12a基因敲除系统，克服了这一自加工限制。研究在多种细胞类型、靶基因及最小化向导RNA文库条件下验证了该平台，实现了pre-crRNA的准确检测与基因编辑。该工作为单细胞多重扰动筛选提供了可靠的新工具，具有广泛的方法学应用价值。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有单细胞扰动筛选（Perturb-seq）主要依赖Cas9，而Cas12a虽适合多重向导RNA表达却因pre-crRNA自我加工特性而难以在单细胞RNA测序中准确恢复序列。本文通过优化pre-crRNA表达载体并构建基于降解子的增强型Cas12a基因敲除系统，克服了这一自加工限制。研究在多种细胞类型、靶基因及最小化向导RNA文库条件下验证了该平台，实现了pre-crRNA的准确检测与基因编辑。该工作为单细胞多重扰动筛选提供了可靠的新工具，具有广泛的方法学应用价值。

deep_dive.title: An emergent disease-associated motor neuron state precedes cell death in ALS.
deep_dive.abstract: To define molecular determinants of motor neuron degeneration in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS), we generated longitudinal single-nucleus transcriptomes and chromatin accessibility profiles of spinal motor neurons together with spatial transcriptomics from the SOD1-G93A mouse model. Vulnerable alpha motor neurons showed thousands of molecular changes, marking a transition into a distinct cell state we named "disease-associated motor neurons" (DMs). We identified transcription factor networks that govern how healthy cells transition into DMs and those associated with motor neuron subtype-selective vulnerability. Upregulation of DM-associated transcription factors in human motor neurons induced key features of DMs, demonstrating an active regulatory component. Human ALS spinal cord single-nucleus RNA sequencing data demonstrated conservation of the DM signature in alpha motor neurons, and human orthologs of regions differentially accessible in SOD1-G93A mouse motor neurons were enriched for ALS genetic risk variants. Together, these findings establish a conserved, genetically linked motor neuron signature in ALS.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: characterizes disease-associated motor neuron states in ALS using single-nucleus transcriptomics and spatial transcriptomics
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用单核转录组与空间转录组研究ALS中疾病相关运动神经元状态，紧扣ALS疾病建模主题
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用单核转录组与空间转录组研究ALS中疾病相关运动神经元状态，紧扣ALS疾病建模主题
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用单核转录组与空间转录组研究ALS中疾病相关运动神经元状态，紧扣ALS疾病建模主题
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This study defines molecular drivers of motor neuron degeneration in ALS using longitudinal single-nucleus transcriptomics, chromatin profiling, and spatial transcriptomics in the SOD1-G93A mouse model. It identifies a disease-associated motor neuron (DM) state and the transcription factor networks driving the transition, validated by inducing DM features in human motor neurons. This advances mechanistic understanding relevant to ALS disease modeling and motor neuron vulnerability.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究针对ALS中运动神经元退变的分子机制不明这一问题，利用SOD1-G93A小鼠模型的纵向单核转录组、染色质可及性及空间转录组数据进行系统刻画。研究方法结合单细胞多组学与空间转录组技术，识别出一种称为疾病相关运动神经元（DM）的新细胞状态及其调控转录因子网络。实验结果表明上调相关转录因子可在人运动神经元中诱导出DM关键特征，验证了调控网络的因果作用。该工作为ALS运动神经元易感性和疾病建模提供了重要的分子机制基础，对ALS相关类器官与疾病模型研究具有直接参考价值。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该研究针对ALS中运动神经元退变的分子机制不明这一问题，利用SOD1-G93A小鼠模型的纵向单核转录组、染色质可及性及空间转录组数据进行系统刻画。研究方法结合单细胞多组学与空间转录组技术，识别出一种称为疾病相关运动神经元（DM）的新细胞状态及其调控转录因子网络。实验结果表明上调相关转录因子可在人运动神经元中诱导出DM关键特征，验证了调控网络的因果作用。该工作为ALS运动神经元易感性和疾病建模提供了重要的分子机制基础，对ALS相关类器官与疾病模型研究具有直接参考价值。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 渐冻症类器官建模 - 聚焦ALS疾病模型与类器官, especially work that connects or combines: amyotrophic lateral sclerosis organoid; ALS organoid model; motor neuron organoid ALS; neuromuscular organoid ALS; ALS disease modeling organoid; iPSC-derived motor neuron ALS; ALS patient-derived organoid; motor neuron disease organoid; ALS neurodegeneration organoid; ALS drug screening organoid.

quick_skim.title: Designing complex organoids through developmental principles.
quick_skim.abstract: Principles of developmental biology have inspired efforts for directed differentiation of human pluripotent stem cells (hPSCs), leading to the first generation of organoids that are now well established as models of human development and disease. However, first-generation organoid models were missing many cell types that would be needed to study normal and pathological processes. Here, we discuss how designing next-generation organoids with increased cellular complexity has been possible by better reproducing developmental processes in play during organogenesis in vivo. We focus on recent conceptual and technical advances in reconstructing appropriate cellular diversity in organoids, dissecting the importance of tissue-tissue interactions and specialized cell addition, and how engineering technologies can further enhance our ability to control how cells are brought together to mimic human development in vitro.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: next-generation organoid design via developmental principles and directed hPSC differentiation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于发育原理设计下一代类器官及hPSC定向分化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于发育原理设计下一代类器官及hPSC定向分化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于发育原理设计下一代类器官及hPSC定向分化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This review addresses the limitation that first-generation organoids lack the cellular complexity needed to model normal and pathological processes. It discusses how developmental biology principles guide directed differentiation of pluripotent stem cells to build next-generation organoids with greater cellular diversity. It focuses on tissue-tissue interactions and specialized cell types, contributing conceptual and technical advances broadly relevant to organoid modeling including neural organoids.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该综述针对第一代类器官模型细胞类型多样性不足、难以完整模拟正常发育与病理过程的问题。作者讨论了如何借助发育生物学原理指导人多能干细胞的定向分化，从而设计具有更高细胞复杂性的下一代类器官。文章重点介绍了重建组织间相互作用和特化细胞类型方面的最新概念与技术进展。这些进展为包括脑类器官在内的复杂类器官模型构建提供了通用设计思路和方法学基础。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该综述针对第一代类器官模型细胞类型多样性不足、难以完整模拟正常发育与病理过程的问题。作者讨论了如何借助发育生物学原理指导人多能干细胞的定向分化，从而设计具有更高细胞复杂性的下一代类器官。文章重点介绍了重建组织间相互作用和特化细胞类型方面的最新概念与技术进展。这些进展为包括脑类器官在内的复杂类器官模型构建提供了通用设计思路和方法学基础。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 神经/脑类器官 - 广义通用覆盖（脑类器官、assembloid、成熟与血管化、类器官芯片、神经环路）, especially work that connects or combines: brain organoid; cerebral organoid; neural organoid; cortical organoid; midbrain organoid; forebrain organoid; assembloid; brain assembloid; neural assembloid; cortical assembloid.

quick_skim.title: Co-expression-based models improve eQTL predictions for transcriptome-wide association studies and highlight new schizophrenia-associated genes.
quick_skim.abstract: Most genetic variants associated with complex heritability phenotypes lie in non-coding regions and are thought to influence disease risk by regulating gene expression. However, most transcriptome-wide association approaches primarily model local (cis) genetic effects, leaving much of gene regulation unexplained. Here, we show that incorporating distal (trans) regulatory effects improves the prediction of gene expression and the identification of disease-associated genes. Using RNA sequencing data from six human post-mortem brain regions, we developed INGENE and MODULE, two models capturing the combined influence of candidate trans-acting variants within gene coexpression networks. Integrating these models with conventional cis-based predictors improved gene expression imputation (maximum likelihood estimation, α = 0.05) for 18,744 genes across regions. Applying this framework to Psychiatric Genomics Consortium wave 3 genotypes identified 766 genes associated with schizophrenia (PFDR < 0.01), including 641 not previously reported by transcriptome-wide analyses. These findings highlight the contribution of distal regulatory mechanisms and gene network interactions to schizophrenia risk.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Identifies new schizophrenia-associated genes via trans-eQTL modeling in human brain tissue, relevant to schizophrenia disease model research
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过反式eQTL建模在人脑组织中发现新的精神分裂症相关基因，与精神分裂症疾病模型研究相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过反式eQTL建模在人脑组织中发现新的精神分裂症相关基因，与精神分裂症疾病模型研究相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过反式eQTL建模在人脑组织中发现新的精神分裂症相关基因，与精神分裂症疾病模型研究相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper addresses the limitation that transcriptome-wide association studies mainly capture cis-regulatory effects, missing trans contributions to gene expression and disease risk. It develops INGENE and MODULE, co-expression network-based models incorporating trans-acting variant effects using RNA-seq from six human postmortem brain regions. The improved eQTL predictions boost identification of disease-associated genes and highlight new schizophrenia risk genes. This advances genetic understanding of schizophrenia pathogenesis via brain transcriptomic modeling.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究针对现有转录组关联分析主要建模顺式调控效应、忽视反式调控对基因表达和疾病风险贡献的局限性。作者利用六个人脑区域的RNA测序数据，开发了INGENE和MODULE两种基于共表达网络的模型，整合候选反式作用变异的综合影响以提升基因表达预测。结果显示该方法改善了eQTL预测并鉴定出新的精神分裂症相关基因。该工作为精神分裂症等复杂疾病的遗传调控机制研究提供了新的生物信息学工具和候选基因。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该研究针对现有转录组关联分析主要建模顺式调控效应、忽视反式调控对基因表达和疾病风险贡献的局限性。作者利用六个人脑区域的RNA测序数据，开发了INGENE和MODULE两种基于共表达网络的模型，整合候选反式作用变异的综合影响以提升基因表达预测。结果显示该方法改善了eQTL预测并鉴定出新的精神分裂症相关基因。该工作为精神分裂症等复杂疾病的遗传调控机制研究提供了新的生物信息学工具和候选基因。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 精神分裂症模型 (Schizophrenia model), especially work that connects or combines: schizophrenia; SCZ; psychiatric disorder model; brain organoid; cerebral organoid; neural organoid; iPSC-derived neuron; cortical organoid; synaptic dysfunction; excitation-inhibition imbalance.

quick_skim.title: SPACE-seq integrates spatial transcriptomics and lineage tracing in native tissues.
quick_skim.abstract: Understanding how cells change state, interact with their neighbors, and organize into tissues requires recording of cellular lineage history in native spatial context. Here, we present SPACE-seq (spatial tracing enabled by CRISPR-based barcodes and slide-seq), a versatile platform that integrates CRISPR-based lineage recording with spatial transcriptomics to jointly resolve lineage, cell state, and tissue architecture at near-cellular resolution in situ. Using SPACE-seq, we uncovered intratumor transcriptional diversification among clonally related cells and identified tumor-stroma crosstalk that reciprocally reshapes behaviors of both malignant and stromal populations, which we further experimentally validated. Beyond disease, SPACE-seq revealed a narrow developmental window in which hepatoblast dispersion contributes to spatially confined lineage compartments that prefigure liver lobar architecture. Together, these results highlight the broad applicability and adaptability of SPACE-seq to uncover previously inaccessible principles of cellular organization, lineage dynamics, and tissue patterning.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: spatial transcriptomics integrated with CRISPR lineage tracing for cell state and tissue architecture
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 空间转录组学结合CRISPR谱系追踪解析细胞状态与组织结构
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 空间转录组学结合CRISPR谱系追踪解析细胞状态与组织结构
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 空间转录组学结合CRISPR谱系追踪解析细胞状态与组织结构
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SPACE-seq combines CRISPR-based lineage recording with spatial transcriptomics to jointly resolve cell lineage, state, and tissue architecture in situ, revealing intratumor diversification and tumor-stroma crosstalk validated experimentally.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究针对如何在原位联合解析细胞谱系、状态与组织空间结构这一难题，提出SPACE-seq平台，将基于CRISPR条形码的谱系记录与slide-seq空间转录组学技术相结合，实现近单细胞分辨率的联合解析。实验中该方法揭示了肿瘤内克隆相关细胞间的转录多样化，并发现了肿瘤与基质间相互重塑彼此行为的信号交流，且通过实验进一步验证。该平台为单细胞与空间生物学研究提供了通用的谱系-空间联合分析工具，具有广泛应用潜力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该研究针对如何在原位联合解析细胞谱系、状态与组织空间结构这一难题，提出SPACE-seq平台，将基于CRISPR条形码的谱系记录与slide-seq空间转录组学技术相结合，实现近单细胞分辨率的联合解析。实验中该方法揭示了肿瘤内克隆相关细胞间的转录多样化，并发现了肿瘤与基质间相互重塑彼此行为的信号交流，且通过实验进一步验证。该平台为单细胞与空间生物学研究提供了通用的谱系-空间联合分析工具，具有广泛应用潜力。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 单细胞 / 生信 - 精简，偏向神经与类器官相关的单细胞分析, especially work that connects or combines: single-cell RNA-seq; single-cell multiomics; spatial transcriptomics; cell type annotation; trajectory inference; RNA velocity; brain single-cell atlas; neural developmental trajectory; single-cell foundation model; batch correction integration.
